$comment: Oracle plugin default configuration. SHIPS WITH ZERO ORG-INTERNAL KNOWLEDGE. Every value here is public/generic. To customize for a private org (internal MCP tools, a non-GitHub review/build toolchain, internal chat/ticketing research), create a project-root .oracle/config.json — it deep-merges over this file and wins on every key it sets. See context/config.schema.md.

research.$comment: Tools each researcher may use per evidence medium. Defaults are public only. A private .oracle/config.json adds internal MCP tools (code search, wiki, chat, ticketing).

operations.$comment: How to do things in THIS repo. Pre-flight detects most of these from project markers (package.json, Cargo.toml, go.mod, pyproject.toml). Override any that detection gets wrong.

pr.$comment: The review/CR commands for THIS repo. Shipped default is GitHub via the public gh CLI. A private org overrides these in its project .oracle/config.json with its own review-CLI commands — the commands ARE the review path, so there is no separate vcs/tool-name key to keep in sync. create SHOULD open a draft so reviewers aren't notified prematurely.
pr.status: gh pr checks --watch=false; gh pr view --json statusCheckRollup,reviewDecision,comments

thresholds.$comment: Run-shape knobs the orchestrator reads. Quality bars (coverage %, mutation %) live in the coding standards, not here.
thresholds.$comment_debate: min_debate_rounds is the floor the orchestrator MAY reduce critic/tester debate toward ONLY for a measured trivially-small, low-risk blast radius (single file; no public surface / wire / concurrency / security). Breadth (lens audit + all gates) never scales down. See context/principles.md 'Full pipeline, every task'.
thresholds.$comment_loops: Every pipeline loop has its OWN ceiling so one loop's churn can't mask another's runaway: max_debate_rounds (consecutive propose-attack-judge rounds per gate), max_verdict_reaudits (judge<->verdict-auditor re-judges on one decision), max_backward_routes (total earlier-phase routes across the run), max_ship_fix_rounds (releaser<->engineer CR-fix round-trips on the CR). Crossing any → ESCALATE. See context/quality-gates.md Loop budgets.

policy.$comment: Hard guardrails. off_limits paths are never written by any agent. The plugin's own internals are off-limits so a run targeting another repo can't mutate Oracle itself.
